name: shadcn/ui
description: A collection of reusable components built with Radix UI and Tailwind CSS. Components are copied into your project, not installed as a dependency.

color.description: Color tokens using CSS custom properties. Values shown are the default light theme.

primary.description: Primary brand color used for prominent interactive elements and emphasis.

secondary.description: Secondary color for less prominent interactive elements.

muted.description: Muted background color for subtle UI areas.

muted-foreground.description: Text color for de-emphasized or secondary text.

accent.description: Accent color for highlights and hover states.

destructive.description: Color for destructive actions such as delete or remove.

ring.description: Focus ring color for interactive elements.

spacing.description: Spacing scale based on a 4px (0.25rem) base unit at the default root font size.

sp-1.description: 4px. Smallest spacing unit, used for tight internal padding.

sp-2.description: 8px. Small spacing, used for gaps between related elements.

sp-4.description: 16px. Standard spacing for padding and margins.

sp-8.description: 32px. Large spacing for major section separation.

radius-sm.description: Smaller border radius for nested or compact elements.

radius-lg.description: Larger border radius for prominent containers.

button.name: Button
button.description: An interactive element that triggers an action when activated. Built on a native button element with support for Radix UI Slot composition via asChild.
button.whenToUse: Use for primary actions, form submissions, dialog confirmations, and any interaction that performs an operation. Choose the variant that matches the action's importance and tone.
button.whenNotToUse: Do not use for navigation between pages or views. Use a Link component or anchor element instead. Do not use for toggling state; use Toggle or Switch.

variant.description: Visual treatment. Use destructive for irreversible actions, ghost for toolbar actions, link for inline text actions.

size.description: Button size. Use icon for square icon-only buttons.

disabled.description: Whether the button is non-interactive. Conveys that the action is unavailable.

asChild.description: When true, merges props and behavior onto the child element instead of rendering a <button>. Used for composing with router Link components.

alert-dialog.name: Alert Dialog
alert-dialog.description: A modal dialog that interrupts the user with important content and expects a response. Built on Radix UI AlertDialog. Renders with a required action and a cancel option. The user cannot dismiss it by clicking the overlay or pressing Escape — they must choose an explicit action. Composed of sub-components: AlertDialog (root), AlertDialogTrigger, AlertDialogContent, AlertDialogHeader, AlertDialogTitle, AlertDialogDescription, AlertDialogFooter, AlertDialogAction, and AlertDialogCancel.
alert-dialog.whenToUse: Use for confirming destructive or irreversible actions: deleting records, discarding unsaved changes, revoking access. Use when the consequence of proceeding is significant enough that the user should explicitly acknowledge it.
alert-dialog.whenNotToUse: Do not use for informational messages or non-destructive confirmations. Use Dialog for those. Do not use when the user should be able to dismiss without choosing — that is what Dialog provides.

open.description: Controlled open state of the dialog.

onOpenChange.description: Callback fired when the open state changes.

dialog.name: Dialog
dialog.description: A modal window that appears over the page content. Built on Radix UI Dialog. Can be dismissed by clicking the overlay, pressing Escape, or activating a close button. Composed of sub-components: Dialog (root), DialogTrigger, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogDescription, DialogFooter, and DialogClose.
dialog.whenToUse: Use for forms, settings panels, detail views, or any content that benefits from focused attention without leaving the current page. Appropriate when the user should be able to dismiss the content without taking an action.
dialog.whenNotToUse: Do not use for confirming destructive actions. Use AlertDialog instead — it prevents accidental dismissal. Do not use for simple, brief messages; use a toast or inline alert.

open.description: Controlled open state of the dialog.

onOpenChange.description: Callback fired when the open state changes.

modal.description: Whether the dialog blocks interaction with the rest of the page.

card.name: Card
card.description: A container for grouping related content and actions. Provides visual separation through a bordered surface. Composed of sub-components: Card (root), CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent, and CardFooter.
card.whenToUse: Use to group related information into a distinct visual unit. Appropriate for dashboard widgets, summary panels, list items with detail, or any content that benefits from visual containment.
card.whenNotToUse: Do not nest Cards inside other Cards. If content hierarchy is needed, use headings and spacing within a single Card. Do not use as a clickable element — wrap content in an interactive element instead.

input.name: Input
input.description: A single-line text input field built on the native <input> element. Supports all standard HTML input types.
input.whenToUse: Use for short-form text entry: names, email addresses, search queries, numeric values. Choose the appropriate HTML type attribute for the content (email, password, number, etc.).
input.whenNotToUse: Do not use for multi-line text. Use Textarea instead. Do not use for selecting from a predefined set of options; use Select or RadioGroup.

type.description: HTML input type (text, email, password, number, search, etc.).

placeholder.description: Placeholder text shown when the input is empty.

badge.name: Badge
badge.description: A small label for categorization, status indication, or metadata. Renders as an inline element.
badge.whenToUse: Use to display status (active, pending, archived), categories, tags, or counts. Appropriate for metadata that should be scannable at a glance.
badge.whenNotToUse: Do not use as a button or interactive element. If the badge should trigger an action, wrap it in a button or use a different component.

variant.description: Visual treatment. Use destructive to indicate errors or critical status.

dropdown-menu.name: Dropdown Menu
dropdown-menu.description: A menu that appears on activation of a trigger element. Built on Radix UI DropdownMenu. Supports items, checkboxes, radio groups, sub-menus, separators, and keyboard navigation. Composed of sub-components: DropdownMenu (root), DropdownMenuTrigger, DropdownMenuContent, DropdownMenuItem, DropdownMenuCheckboxItem, DropdownMenuRadioGroup, DropdownMenuRadioItem, DropdownMenuLabel, DropdownMenuSeparator, DropdownMenuGroup, and DropdownMenuSub.
dropdown-menu.whenToUse: Use when a set of actions or options needs to be accessible from a single trigger without taking up permanent screen space. Appropriate for overflow menus, context-sensitive actions, and settings with multiple choices.
dropdown-menu.whenNotToUse: Do not use for navigation — use a navigation menu or links. Do not use when the options should always be visible; use RadioGroup or a set of Buttons instead.

onOpenChange.description: Callback fired when the open state changes.

modal.description: Whether the menu blocks interaction with the rest of the page.

patterns.name: Destructive Action Confirmation
patterns.description: A confirmation flow for actions that are irreversible or have significant consequences, using AlertDialog to require explicit user acknowledgment.
patterns.intent: Prevent accidental data loss or irreversible state changes by requiring the user to explicitly confirm before proceeding.
patterns.context: Use whenever an action cannot be undone: deleting a record, removing a team member, clearing form data, revoking access, or any operation the user might regret if triggered accidentally.
patterns.guidance: Use AlertDialog, not Dialog, for destructive confirmations. The trigger should clearly indicate the destructive nature of the action. Inside the AlertDialog, provide a clear title stating what will happen, a description of the consequences, and two actions: a cancel option and a confirm option. The confirm button MUST use the destructive variant. Place the cancel action before the confirm action in the footer. The description should state specifically what will be affected (e.g., 'This will permanently delete 3 projects and all associated data').

patterns.name: Form Field Layout
patterns.description: Standard arrangement of form controls within a Card container, providing consistent structure for data entry interfaces.
patterns.intent: Give forms a consistent visual structure that groups related inputs, separates them from surrounding content, and provides clear hierarchy through Card sub-components.
patterns.context: Use for settings panels, profile editors, checkout flows, or any interface where the user enters structured data across multiple fields.
patterns.guidance: Wrap the form in a Card. Use CardHeader with CardTitle and CardDescription to introduce the form's purpose. Place inputs inside CardContent with consistent vertical spacing (space-y-4 or equivalent). Group related fields together. Place primary actions (submit, save) in CardFooter. Labels should be associated with inputs for accessibility. Required fields should be visually indicated.

patterns.name: Contextual Actions Menu
patterns.description: A pattern for exposing a set of context-sensitive actions through a dropdown menu triggered by an icon button.
patterns.intent: Keep interfaces clean by collecting secondary or overflow actions behind a single trigger, while maintaining discoverability through a familiar interaction pattern.
patterns.context: Use for row-level actions in tables or lists, overflow menus in toolbars, or any place where multiple actions apply to a single item but showing all of them inline would be visually noisy.
patterns.guidance: Use a Button with the ghost variant and icon size as the DropdownMenuTrigger. A vertical ellipsis icon is the conventional trigger for contextual menus. Group related actions with DropdownMenuGroup and separate groups with DropdownMenuSeparator. Place destructive actions (delete, remove) at the end of the menu, visually distinguished. Include keyboard shortcuts in DropdownMenuItem when available.

antiPatterns.name: Using Dialog for Destructive Confirmations
antiPatterns.description: Using the dismissible Dialog component instead of AlertDialog when confirming a destructive or irreversible action.
antiPatterns.reason: Dialog can be dismissed by clicking the overlay or pressing Escape, which means a user can accidentally bypass the confirmation without making a conscious choice. AlertDialog forces the user to select an explicit action (confirm or cancel), providing a stronger safeguard against accidental destructive operations. This distinction also matters for accessibility: screen readers announce AlertDialog with greater urgency.

antiPatterns.name: Nesting Interactive Elements
antiPatterns.description: Placing a clickable or focusable element (Button, Link, Input) inside another clickable element (a clickable Card, a Button, or a Link).
antiPatterns.reason: Nested interactive elements create ambiguous click targets and are a significant accessibility violation. Screen readers cannot determine user intent when interactive elements are nested, and the resulting behavior varies unpredictably across browsers. The outer element may intercept clicks intended for the inner element, or both may fire, leading to unexpected navigation or duplicate actions.

antiPatterns.name: Using a Styled Div as a Button
antiPatterns.description: Creating a clickable element by adding an onClick handler and visual styles to a <div> or <span> instead of using the Button component or a native <button> element.
antiPatterns.reason: A styled div lacks keyboard accessibility (not focusable by default, does not respond to Enter or Space), is not announced as a button by screen readers, and does not participate in form submission. The Button component handles all of these concerns. Using a div requires manually re-implementing focus management, keyboard handling, and ARIA attributes — work that is error-prone and already solved by the component.

react.name: React
react.description: React components built with Radix UI primitives and styled with Tailwind CSS. Components are copied into your project source rather than imported from a package — you own and can modify the code.
react.guidance: Run 'npx shadcn@latest init' to set up the project, then add individual components with 'npx shadcn@latest add <component>'. Components are placed in your project (typically src/components/ui/ or components/ui/) and can be freely modified. Tailwind CSS v4 and a path alias (usually @/) are expected.

button.guidance: Uses class-variance-authority (cva) for variant styles. Supports Radix UI Slot via the asChild prop for composing with router Link components.

alert-dialog.guidance: Exports multiple sub-components: AlertDialog, AlertDialogTrigger, AlertDialogContent, AlertDialogHeader, AlertDialogTitle, AlertDialogDescription, AlertDialogFooter, AlertDialogAction, AlertDialogCancel. Built on @radix-ui/react-alert-dialog.

dialog.guidance: Exports multiple sub-components: Dialog, DialogTrigger, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogDescription, DialogFooter, DialogClose. Built on @radix-ui/react-dialog.

card.guidance: Exports Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent, CardFooter. Pure HTML/CSS components — no Radix dependency.

input.guidance: A styled native <input> element. Forwards all standard HTML input attributes via React.forwardRef.

badge.guidance: Uses cva for variant styles. Renders as a <div> by default.

dropdown-menu.guidance: Exports multiple sub-components: DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuTrigger, DropdownMenuContent, DropdownMenuItem, DropdownMenuCheckboxItem, DropdownMenuRadioGroup, DropdownMenuRadioItem, DropdownMenuLabel, DropdownMenuSeparator, DropdownMenuGroup, DropdownMenuSub, DropdownMenuSubTrigger, DropdownMenuSubContent. Built on @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu.
